company.what_they_do: RedCentric (redcentric.site) presents itself as a professional recruitment and staffing agency offering worldwide placement solutions with a stated focus on 'strategic hiring for long-term business growth.' This is a distinct and separate entity from the well-known UK IT managed service provider Redcentric PLC (redcentricplc.com), which should not be confused with the signup company.
company.raw_notes: The website (redcentric.site) is extremely sparse — the only publicly visible content is a headline reading 'Professional Recruitment & Staffing Solutions Worldwide' and a subline 'Strategic hiring for long-term business growth.' No About page, no team page, no service detail pages, no client logos, no blog, and no verifiable founding date or location were found. The domain uses the .site TLD rather than a more established extension (.com, .co.uk), which is notable for a claimed worldwide staffing firm. The site has very little indexed content and no third-party coverage or reviews. It should NOT be confused with Redcentric PLC (redcentricplc.com), a legitimate, publicly listed UK IT MSP founded in 1997 with ~450 employees, headquartered in Harrogate.

individual.name: James (last name unknown)
individual.notes: No individual named 'James Addam' or any close variant could be found associated with redcentric.site through web search. The email address (admamjames@redcentric.site) has an unusual prefix — 'admamjames' — which appears to be a scrambled or reversed arrangement of 'james addam' (itself a non-standard spelling of 'James Adam/Adams'). No LinkedIn profile, company team page, press mention, or other first-party source confirms this person's identity, role, or affiliation with the company. The combination of an obscure domain, near-empty website, and an anomalous email prefix pattern warrants caution.
